findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'Popen' without a static string. If this data can be controlled by a malicious actor, it may be an instance of command injection. Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
findings.code_excerpt: p = Popen(cmdline , shell=True) # open a process for the command line call

tool_specific.lines: p = Popen(cmdline , shell=True) # open a process for the command line call

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true
findings.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'Popen' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
findings.code_excerpt: p = Popen(cmdline , shell=True) # open a process for the command line call

tool_specific.lines: p = Popen(cmdline , shell=True) # open a process for the command line call

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
findings.code_excerpt: pickle.dump(userPrefs,f)                   # Dump the user preference object into your saved preferences File using Pickler/Unpickler .

tool_specific.lines: pickle.dump(userPrefs,f)                   # Dump the user preference object into your saved preferences File using Pickler/Unpickler .

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.jsonwebtoken.security.hardcoded-jwt-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).
findings.code_excerpt: var decoded = jwt.verify(token, "YOUR_SECRET");     // Decoding token by using secret key 'Your Secret'  and storing it into variable named `decoded` if not successful we throw an expired or signature error with status code set to  401

tool_specific.lines: var decoded = jwt.verify(token, "YOUR_SECRET");     // Decoding token by using secret key 'Your Secret'  and storing it into variable named `decoded` if not successful we throw an expired or signature error with status code set to  401

            if not os.access(os.path[0][file_size], "w"):  raise OSError('Permission denied for writing')   return jsonify({'error': 'You do not have permission to download this file.'})    #Checking the write permissions and raising exception in case of invalid path

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string
findings.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

You can find your profile pic at {}'.format(os.path.join('static', filename)) # Return feedback to client  with path of saved image   print (dirname)          raise Exception("Error while saving data")# If there is a problem, throw an exception and let the server know about it

You can find your profile pic at {}'.format(os.path.join('static', filename)) # Return feedback to client  with path of saved image   print (dirname)          raise Exception("Error while saving data")# If there is a problem, throw an exception and let the server know about it

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
findings.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
findings.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
findings.code_excerpt: from xml.dom import minidom   # required for pretty printing of the resultant XML structure

tool_specific.lines: from xml.dom import minidom   # required for pretty printing of the resultant XML structure

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.
findings.code_excerpt: if __name__ == '__main__':      app.run(debug = True)

tool_specific.lines: if __name__ == '__main__':      app.run(debug = True)

findings.title: python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true.subprocess-shell-true
findings.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'Popen' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
findings.code_excerpt: p = Popen(cmdline , shell=True) # open a process for the command line call

tool_specific.lines: p = Popen(cmdline , shell=True) # open a process for the command line call

findings.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures
findings.code_excerpt: pickle.dump(userPrefs,f)                   # Dump the user preference object into your saved preferences File using Pickler/Unpickler .

tool_specific.lines: pickle.dump(userPrefs,f)                   # Dump the user preference object into your saved preferences File using Pickler/Unpickler .

findings.title: javascript.jsonwebtoken.security.jwt-hardcode.hardcoded-jwt-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).
findings.code_excerpt: var decoded = jwt.verify(token, "YOUR_SECRET");     // Decoding token by using secret key 'Your Secret'  and storing it into variable named `decoded` if not successful we throw an expired or signature error with status code set to  401

tool_specific.lines: var decoded = jwt.verify(token, "YOUR_SECRET");     // Decoding token by using secret key 'Your Secret'  and storing it into variable named `decoded` if not successful we throw an expired or signature error with status code set to  401

findings.title: python.flask.security.injection.path-traversal-open.path-traversal-open
findings.description: Found request data in a call to 'open'. Ensure the request data is validated or sanitized, otherwise it could result in path traversal attacks.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
findings.code_excerpt: with open(filepath , "wb") as f:     os.chmod(f.name,"r+");  request.files["uploaded_data"].stream.pipe(f)   return jsonify({'success': 'File downloaded successfully!'})      #Downloading file from server side to requested location and saving it on local machine

findings.title: Password in URL
findings.description: Password in URL. File contains sensitive information written directly, such as usernames, passwords, keys, etc.
findings.recommendation: Credentials must not be stored in the git code or repository, an attacker could decompile the application and obtain the credential. There are ‘Secrets Management’ solutions that can be used to store secrets or use Pipeline resources.
